seed_problem: Assign frequencies to transmitters minimizing total interference conflicts under a channel budget; checker scores baseline_conflicts / achieved.
mutation: composite: generalize the input from pure co-channel conflicts to a frequency-assignment model with both co-channel (full weight) and adjacent-channel (half weight) leakage on a general weighted overlap graph with embedded over-budget cliques, and grade by baseline/achieved interference cost so optimal labeling is NP-hard and greedy / sequential-min-conflict / local-search strategies diverge.
title: Static on the Long Arm: Interstellar Relay Channel Assignment
strategies: all-same-channel baseline: tune every station to channel 1 (feasible, equals the scoring baseline B)
sequential min-conflict greedy: process stations in order, assign each the channel minimizing added co-/adjacent-channel crosstalk against already-assigned neighbors
local search with random restarts: greedy or random init, then repeatedly re-tune each station to its locally best channel until stable, keeping the best of several seeded restarts
structure-aware coloring: color the embedded over-budget cliques first / spread heavy-weight overlaps across channels to avoid co-channel and adjacent-channel penalties
